_description: Structured database of abstraction failure and success patterns. See project_spec.md §10.4 for schema and §12 for taxonomy framework. v2.0 adds P004/P006/P008, fixes P007 misclassification, adds decision_framework and portability_matrix.
_changelog: v2.0 (2026-03-22): Added P004 (API Expressivity Gap), P006 (Tiling Policy Overhead), P008 (Level-Set Dispatch Amplification). Moved P007 from incorrect success_patterns to patterns array. Added decision_framework section with five calibrated rules, launch overhead thresholds, and validated examples. Added portability_matrix with harmonic-mean phi values across NVIDIA RTX 5060 and AMD MI300X.

patterns.name: Launch Overhead Dominance
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 30-60% slower than native for kernels with execution time < 50 µs
patterns.root_cause_detail: Extra function call layers not fully inlined; runtime dispatch overhead accumulates when kernel execution time is short
affected_workloads: irregular applications with many small launches
mitigation: Use native for inner-loop hotspots
Coarsen task granularity to amortize launch cost
Enable aggressive inlining flags (-O3 --inline-aggressive)

evidence.note: Julia small (N=32³) eff=0.70 and medium (N=128³) eff=0.69 are consistent with @cuda launch overhead (~0.2-0.5ms) dominating short kernel times (N=32 kernel time ≈0.014ms, N=128 ≈0.16ms). Large N eff=1.376 is a thermal artifact. RAJA small eff=0.94 confirms penalty is Julia-specific, not kernel-size-specific for all abstractions. Canonical AMD example: SYCL E1 small (N=1M) eff=0.167 (527 vs 3154 GB/s native).

patterns.name: Compiler Backend Unrolling Failure
patterns.symptom: Abstraction 2-4x slower on NVIDIA for nested loops; matches on AMD
patterns.root_cause_detail: NVCC/PTX backend fails to unroll template-instantiated loops correctly; AMD LLVM backend handles the same patterns without regression
mitigation: Manual loop unrolling with #pragma unroll
Use vendor-tuned library (cuBLAS) for the failing kernel
File compiler bug report with minimal reproducer

evidence.note: Benchmark eff=0.24 is thermally contaminated (sequential session: 2.76x thermal ratio confirmed by fresh-state nsys profiling). Thermal-corrected eff=0.66. True root cause is P004 API Limitation (RAJA forall cannot express shared-memory tiling); P002 Compiler Backend Failure not confirmed. cudaLaunchKernel overhead is 0.05% of runtime. P002 pending ncu hardware counters (RmProfilingAdminOnly=1 blocks hardware PMU on RTX 5060).

patterns.literature: Godoy et al. 2023 — Julia DGEMM on A100

patterns.name: Memory Layout Mismatch
patterns.symptom: 20-40% bandwidth reduction vs. native; L2 hit rate drops from ~75% to ~45%
patterns.root_cause_detail: Abstraction defaults to non-optimal array layout (e.g., LayoutRight when LayoutLeft required for NVIDIA coalescing); causes strided instead of unit-stride access
affected_workloads: multi-dimensional arrays with high data reuse
mitigation: Explicitly specify Kokkos::LayoutLeft for NVIDIA kernels
Profile L2 hit rate before and after layout change
Document layout requirements per platform in config.yaml

evidence.note: Kokkos small eff=0.76. RAJA small eff=0.94 with identical kernel logic but direct block/thread mapping — P003 layout mismatch NOT confirmed. Kokkos uses MDRangePolicy<Rank<3>> with tiling hint {2,4,32}; tiling overhead is more likely explanation (P006). Kokkos large eff=0.703 is thermally biased. P003 remains unconfirmed in E3.

patterns.name: API Expressivity Gap
patterns.symptom: Abstraction API cannot express the optimal algorithm; forces naive O(N³) global-memory-only access despite native achieving O(N²) memory complexity with shared-memory tiling
patterns.root_cause_detail: RAJA forall() and Julia @cuda/@roc macros lack scoped shared-memory primitives for manual tiling. DGEMM requires shared-memory double-buffered tiles for near-peak FLOP/s; without them, the abstraction falls back to global-memory-only access with O(N³) DRAM traffic vs O(N²) for tiled native. Distinct from P003 (layout) and P001 (launch overhead): P004 is algorithmic incompleteness, not a runtime cost. Kokkos TeamPolicy and SYCL local_accessor both provide shared-memory equivalents and achieve excellent PPC on the same kernel where RAJA and Julia fail.
mitigation: Use vendor-tuned library (cuBLAS, rocBLAS) for tiled compute kernels
Kokkos TeamPolicy with scratch-pad memory allocation is the correct Kokkos workaround
SYCL local_accessor provides shared-memory equivalent — P004 does not apply to SYCL
Accept PPC 0.19-0.35 for RAJA/Julia on tiled compute kernels or switch abstraction

evidence.note: Kokkos TeamPolicy and SYCL local_accessor both achieve excellent PPC (0.91-0.99) on the same DGEMM kernel where RAJA and Julia fail (0.19-0.32). This isolates the cause to shared-memory expressivity, not abstraction overhead. The pattern is size-independent on AMD: RAJA PPC does not improve with N (0.316→0.236→0.189), confirming algorithmic root cause. Note: P004 absent on NVIDIA for Julia due to P005 (column-major layout advantage compensates on RTX 5060). P004 only dominates on AMD where native DGEMM is 4× more performant.

patterns.ppc_impact: 0.19-0.35 for affected abstractions on compute-bound tiled kernels
patterns.literature: Godoy et al. 2023, Pennycook et al. 2021 (roofline for portability)

patterns.name: Layout-Induced Coalescing Advantage
patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency > 1.0 vs native baseline; abstraction outperforms native
patterns.root_cause: Column-major Fortran layout in Julia eliminates transposition overhead; warp-level memory access patterns are superior to row-major C implementations at all compute-bound problem sizes
patterns.root_cause_detail: Julia column-major CuArrays map warp threads (varying threadIdx.x → row) to consecutive elements of the same matrix column, giving perfect coalescing on A and broadcast access on B. The native tiled C kernel uses row-major storage and incurs 512 __syncthreads() barriers at N=8192 (256 tiles × 2 barriers). At arithmetic intensity ≈ 683 FLOP/byte (compute-bound), synchronisation overhead exceeds shared-memory bandwidth savings. Fresh-state nsys profiling confirms the advantage is size-independent: eff≈1.29 at N=4096 (medium) and eff≈1.20-1.25 at N=8192 (large). The apparent non-monotonic benchmark scaling (0.80 medium → 1.25 large) was a thermal contamination artifact, not a size threshold.

evidence.validation_method: CUDA event timing (CUDA.@elapsed) confirms GPU-only kernel time produces same GFLOP/s as wall-clock measurement; max_rel_err < 1e-6 at N=128. Fresh-state nsys profiling confirms medium-N advantage: 225 GFLOP/s fresh vs 139 GFLOP/s benchmark (1.62x thermal ratio). Large-N benchmark/profiling delta is only 4%, confirming large-N result is thermally clean.
evidence.platform_asymmetry: P005 confirmed on NVIDIA RTX 5060 only. Julia naive AMD eff=0.199-0.306 (poor) — column-major layout advantage insufficient when native AMD DGEMM is 4× higher performing baseline.

patterns.implication: Layout conventions can dominate algorithmic overhead at large problem sizes. Expert optimisation assumptions (tiling always improves performance) do not hold in compute-bound regimes where synchronisation overhead dominates.

patterns.name: Tiling Policy Overhead at Small Granularity
patterns.symptom: Kokkos MDRangePolicy 15-25% slower than RAJA forall at small problem sizes; confirmed also by native tile being 15-34% slower than native notile in CSR N-body
patterns.root_cause_detail: Kokkos MDRangePolicy<Rank<3>> with tile hints {2,4,32} requires block boundary handling: tiles that extend beyond grid edges require thread masking. At N=32³, the 32768 active cells are served by ceil(32/2)×ceil(32/4)×ceil(32/32)=128 tiles each of size 2×4×32. Boundary masking overhead constitutes ~20-30% of warp execution cycles at small N. RAJA maps directly to threadIdx without tiling, avoiding this overhead. Confirmed independently by E7: native tiled (TILE_SIZE=24, per-thread shared-memory staging) is 15-34% SLOWER than native notile for CSR N-body — tiling adds shared-memory pressure and barrier cost when data reuse does not compensate. Distinct from P001: overhead is block scheduling complexity, not launch latency.
mitigation: Reduce tile size proportionally to problem size: {1,1,32} for N=32 avoids boundary overhead
Switch to RangePolicy + manual 3D index arithmetic for small N
Profile-guided tile selection: T = min(N/8, 32)

e3_rtx5060.note: RAJA uses flat RangePolicy + 3D index arithmetic; Kokkos uses MDRangePolicy<Rank<3>>{2,4,32}. Same kernel logic. 0.182 gap attributable to boundary masking overhead at N=32³.

e7_rtx5060_native_tile_vs_notile.note: H1 (tiling always improves performance) NOT confirmed. Per-thread staging in CSR layout adds shared-memory pressure and synchronisation cost without data reuse benefit. Tiling is 15-34% slower than untiled native across all problem sizes.

patterns.validation_note: ncu occupancy analysis at N=32³ pending (RmProfilingAdminOnly=1 blocks hardware PMU on RTX 5060). Partially validated by two independent E3/E7 observations pointing to same mechanism.
patterns.ppc_impact: 0.65-0.85 at small N; recovers to 0.90+ at medium/large N as overhead amortizes

patterns.name: Load Imbalance Amplification
patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency collapses multiplicatively below what P001 Launch Overhead predicts alone
patterns.root_cause_detail: Power-law degree distribution (Pareto γ=2.5) creates extreme row-length variance. One-thread-per-row mapping leaves most warps stalled waiting on the few long-row threads (warp divergence + thread idling). For interpreted runtimes (Julia @cuda), this compounds with P001 Launch Overhead: P001 alone predicts eff≈0.56 for julia/small, but power_law pushes it to 0.45 — a 0.11 multiplicative penalty on top of the 0.44 already from P001. The effect shrinks at larger N as tail rows dominate a smaller fraction of total runtime.
mitigation: Warp-per-row CSR (multiple threads collaborate on one row, reduces idle warps)
CSR segmented reduction (CUB DeviceSegmentedReduce) for load-balanced SpMV
Row reordering to group similar-length rows in the same warp
Use native CUDA for power-law workloads at small N; abstraction safe at large N where collapse is uniform

evidence.note: julia/laplacian/small eff=0.56 is P001 alone (kernel ~0.05ms). julia/power_law/small eff=0.45 — same launch overhead, but power-law nnz variance (max degree ~256, min=1) means most threads idle, amplifying the launch-overhead fraction. Multiplicative compound: P001 factor × P007 factor, not additive sum. Effect decays with N: julia/power_law/large eff=0.91 (both P007 and P001 amortized). RAJA is immune at large N (eff=0.99).

patterns.ppc_impact: 0.40-0.70 at small N with power-law inputs; near-native at large N
patterns.literature: Merrill et al. 2015 (merge-based SpMV), Yang et al. 2018 (design principles for sparse GPU kernels)

patterns.name: Level-Set Dispatch Amplification
patterns.symptom: Abstraction efficiency decays with n_levels (not with N); level-set algorithms accumulate O(n_levels × per_launch_overhead) total penalty
patterns.root_cause_detail: Level-set algorithms (SpTRSV forward substitution, BFS frontier expansion) require one GPU kernel launch per dependency level to maintain correctness. Each abstraction launch carries its per-invocation overhead: Julia @cuda ~300µs, SYCL/HSA ~70µs, Kokkos::fence ~25µs, RAJA::synchronize ~15µs. Total overhead = n_levels × per_launch_overhead, which is workload-structure-dependent, not N-dependent. For Julia/laplacian/large (n_levels=181), accumulated overhead ≈ 181 × 0.3ms ≈ 54ms per solve — dominated over actual compute. Distinct from P001 (single-kernel overhead): P008 overhead grows with n_levels, not with N. Same workload may show P001 or P008 depending on whether n_levels or N drives the overhead. Distinguishing test: fix N, vary n_levels — if efficiency decays with n_levels but not N, pattern is P008.
patterns.inverse_pattern: When levels are WIDE (many rows per level), RAJA and Kokkos exceed native PPC because their fence implementations pipeline better than native hipDeviceSynchronize(). Wide-level parallelism turns P008 from a liability into an advantage: RAJA random/large RTX eff=1.299, AMD eff=1.070.
mitigation: Fuse multiple levels into a single kernel launch where correctness permits (wavefront parallelism with atomic writes)
Prefer RAJA/Kokkos for level-set algorithms: per-launch overhead is 5-25µs vs Julia 300µs
For SYCL: investigate SYCL graph API (persistent kernel graph) to amortize HSA submission overhead across levels
Pre-compute level structure; batch independent rows into coarsened super-levels

e5_julia_rtx5060_laplacian.counterpoint: julia/random/large has N=8192 (same N as laplacian/large) but n_levels=75 and eff=0.897 vs 0.464. Confirms n_levels, not N, as predictor.

e6_julia_rtx5060_graph_comparison.note: 2d_grid (low irregularity=0.57, regular wavefront) is WORSE than erdos_renyi (high irregularity=1.25-1.64). n_levels is the dominant predictor, not frontier irregularity.

e5_sycl_amd_mi300x.note: SYCL near-exact model match: predicted PPC = T_compute/(T_compute + L × 0.07ms) ≈ 0.49 observed. Strongest quantitative validation of R2 decision rule.

e5_raja_kokkos_wide_levels_exceed_native.note: P008 inverse: wide levels (max_level_width=524) mean per-launch overhead negligible vs compute. RAJA/Kokkos synchronize more efficiently than native cudaDeviceSynchronize/hipDeviceSynchronize in pipelined execution, yielding eff > 1.0.

patterns.ppc_impact: Julia: 0.35-0.46 at high n_levels; SYCL: 0.49-0.54; RAJA/Kokkos: 0.80-1.30 depending on level width
patterns.literature: Liu & Vinter 2015 (SpTRSV level-set parallelism), Merrill et al. 2012 (scalable GPU graph traversal)

decision_framework._description: Quantitative rules for predicting abstraction PPC from measurable workload characteristics. Operationalizes Contribution 3: how to quantify the portability-performance trade-off before committing to an abstraction.
decision_framework._status: calibrated on E1-E7 full dataset; formal held-out validation (train E1-E5, predict E6-E7) in progress

predictor_variables.name: kernel_execution_time_us
predictor_variables.description: Single-kernel GPU execution time in microseconds
predictor_variables.how_to_measure: CUDA.@elapsed or hipEventElapsedTime on representative single kernel; median over 30 runs

predictor_variables.name: n_levels
predictor_variables.description: Number of GPU kernel launches required per algorithm invocation (level-set depth for SpTRSV/BFS)
predictor_variables.how_to_measure: CPU-side BFS on matrix/graph level structure; output of level_analysis() in analysis/overhead.py

predictor_variables.name: arithmetic_intensity
predictor_variables.description: Arithmetic intensity of the kernel (roofline x-axis)
predictor_variables.how_to_measure: ncu --metrics or empirical FLOP/memory ratio from analysis/roofline.py

predictor_variables.name: load_imbalance_index
predictor_variables.description: Coefficient of variation of work per thread (row length CV for SpMV/SpTRSV)
predictor_variables.how_to_measure: sigma = std(row_lengths) / mean(row_lengths) from matrix statistics

predictor_variables.name: api_completeness
predictor_variables.description: Whether the abstraction can express the optimal algorithm (shared-memory tiling, warp-level primitives)

launch_overhead_thresholds_us._description: Per-launch effective GPU-side overhead (µs) after JIT warmup. Recalibrated 2026-03-22 from efficiency back-calculation: overhead = T_k × (1-eff)/eff. These are device-side overheads, NOT nsys host-trace times. Previous julia/sycl values were cold-launch nsys host overhead — corrected here.
launch_overhead_thresholds_us._calibration_method: overhead_us = T_k × (1 - eff) / eff at observed efficiency data points; cross-validated across multiple E1-E7 configs

julia_cuda.source: E3 stencil small RTX (T_k=14µs, eff=0.704 → 5.9µs); E7 nbody small RTX (T_k=9µs, eff=0.540 → 7.7µs). Note: 300µs was cold-launch host overhead, not device overhead after warmup.
julia_cuda.level_set_source: E5 SpTRSV laplacian large RTX (eff=0.464, T_k=22.6µs → 25.4µs/level; variable-width dispatch adds overhead per level)

julia_rocm.source: E1 stream small AMD (T_k=3.8µs, eff=0.503 → 3.75µs). Note: 150µs was an incorrect inference.

sycl_hsa.source: E1 stream small AMD (T_k=3.8µs, eff=0.167 → 18.9µs). Note: 70µs was total per-launch wall time including compute, not incremental overhead.

kokkos_cuda.source: E3 stencil small RTX (T_k=14µs, eff=0.760 → 4.4µs); E7 nbody small RTX (T_k=9µs, eff=0.592 → 6.2µs)

raja_cuda.source: E3 stencil small RTX (T_k=14µs, eff=0.942 → 0.9µs); E7 nbody small RTX (T_k=9µs, eff=0.733 → 3.3µs)

decision_rules.name: Launch Overhead Dominance Check
decision_rules.action: Use native for this kernel; coarsen task granularity before adopting abstraction

validated_examples.note: Model underpredicts — Julia CUDA.jl batches some overhead amortization. Prediction is a conservative lower bound.
Model overpredicts — SYCL also has queue management overhead beyond single launch cost.

decision_rules.name: Level-Set Dispatch Budget Check
decision_rules.action: For Julia/SYCL on level-set algorithms: estimate L × overhead before committing. Prefer RAJA or Kokkos for level-set workloads.

validated_examples.note: Observed lower than predicted — T_compute also includes synchronisation stalls.
Near-exact match. Strongest quantitative validation of this rule.

decision_rules.name: API Expressivity Gate
decision_rules.action: Use Kokkos TeamPolicy, SYCL local_accessor, or vendor library (cuBLAS/rocBLAS) instead

validated_examples.note: Size-independent degradation (PPC does not improve with N) confirms algorithmic root cause.
P004 masked on NVIDIA by P005 (column-major layout advantage). P004 only dominates where native baseline is very strong (AMD).

decision_rules.name: Load Imbalance Compound Check
decision_rules.action: Use warp-per-row or segmented reduction; abstraction overhead compounds with imbalance at small N

decision_rules.name: Safe Abstraction Prediction
decision_rules.condition: T_k > 10 × overhead_us[abstraction] AND R3_condition = false AND N is large
decision_rules.action: Abstraction is safe; near-native performance expected. RAJA/Kokkos may exceed 1.0 on wide-level workloads.

validated_examples.note: Exceeds 1.0 due to P008 inverse (sync pipelining advantage)

summary_lookup_table._description: Quick-reference matrix: given (abstraction, workload_class), expected PPC tier

workload_classes.level_set_narrow: level-set algorithm, narrow levels (few rows/level), high n_levels

portability_matrix._description: Cross-platform portability metric phi = harmonic mean of PPC across NVIDIA RTX 5060 and AMD MI300X. phi = 2 / (1/ppc_nvidia + 1/ppc_amd). phi < 1.0 means at least one platform suffers; phi >= 1.0 means both platforms excellent or better.
portability_matrix._interpretation: phi >= 0.90 = portable-excellent; phi 0.70-0.90 = portable-acceptable; phi < 0.70 = portability concern
portability_matrix._note: SYCL phi not computable for E1/E3/E4/E6/E7 as SYCL was not available on RTX 5060 in those experiments

E1_stream_large.note: All phi >= 0.95: memory-bound workloads at large N are portable across both platforms

E2_dgemm_large.note: raja_naive phi=0.272 is the worst portability result in the dataset. P004 API Expressivity Gap is a portability killer. julia_naive phi=0.492 — P005 layout advantage on NVIDIA partially compensates, but not enough.

E3_stencil_medium_n128.note: Medium N chosen as cleanest cross-platform comparison (large N has thermal issues on RTX). Julia phi=0.597: poor portability — P001 on RTX, access pattern mismatch on AMD. RAJA phi=0.830: acceptable, better than Kokkos despite Kokkos being the 'portability' tool.

E5_sptrsv_laplacian_large.note: RAJA phi=0.967: best portability on level-set workloads. Julia phi=0.595: P008 on RTX (n_levels=181 × 300µs) dominates; AMD less severe (150µs Julia ROCm path).

E5_sptrsv_random_large.note: Both phi > 1.0: wide-level workloads with RAJA/Kokkos are portable AND outperform native on both platforms.

abstraction_portability_summary._description: Median phi across all configs where both platform PPC values are available

kokkos.verdict: acceptable portability; strong at memory-bound, weak at small N and complex tiling

raja.verdict: best overall portability; weak only when API expressivity gap applies (P004)

julia.verdict: poor portability; platform-asymmetric behavior (P005 on NVIDIA, P008 on both, P001 on both)

sycl.note: Only AMD data for most kernels; E2 DGEMM AMD excellent but no NVIDIA comparison available

e4.note: AI≈0.13 FLOP/byte — firmly memory-bound. power_law matrix creates extreme load imbalance (Pareto gamma=2.5). P001 and P007 both confirmed.

key_results.kokkos_2d_grid_note: Kokkos 2d_grid AMD eff=332.6 at large N is a native baseline quality issue: native uses sequential-through-small-kernels BFS; Kokkos RangePolicy parallelizes across the full frontier of 256 vertices in a single kernel. This is NOT an abstraction advantage — it is a workload-naive native baseline. The comparison is valid for measuring abstraction overhead but must not be cited as 'abstraction outperforms native' without this qualification.

_notes: v2.0 (2026-03-22): P007 was incorrectly placed in success_patterns array in v1.0. Moved to patterns array. All patterns now in single unified array.
P001 status=validated: canonical worst case is sycl/E1/small/AMD (eff=0.167). Julia E3 small (eff=0.70) is the NVIDIA canonical example.
P002 status=partially_validated: true root cause is P004 (API Limitation), not compiler backend. P002 pending ncu hardware counters on RTX 5060 (RmProfilingAdminOnly=1 blocks PMU).
P003 status=hypothesis: not confirmed in E3. The E3 Kokkos/RAJA gap is better explained by P006 (tiling overhead).
P004 status=validated: size-independent AMD DGEMM failures isolate algorithmic root cause. SYCL and Kokkos both unaffected, confirming shared-memory expressivity as the discriminating factor.
P005 status=validated: julia_naive/large/RTX eff=1.25 (benchmark) and eff=1.20-1.29 (fresh-state) confirmed by GPU event timing. Platform-asymmetric: absent on AMD.
P006 status=partially_validated: E3 Kokkos/RAJA delta at N=32³ and E7 tile-vs-notile both support the mechanism. ncu occupancy analysis pending.
P007 status=validated: compound multiplicative penalty (P001 × P007) confirmed in E4 power_law small configs.

P008 status=validated: best evidence is SYCL/E5/AMD near-exact model match (predicted=0.49, observed=0.49). Julia/laplacian/large (n_levels=181 eff=0.464 vs random/large n_levels=75 eff=0.897 at same N) confirms n_levels as driver.
BFS Kokkos 332x: do NOT cite as abstraction success. Native BFS baseline is workload-naive (not GPU-optimized). This is a methodology note, not a pattern.

decision_framework R1 predictions are conservative lower bounds (Julia CUDA.jl batching not modeled). R2/SYCL/AMD is the most accurate rule (near-exact match). R3 is a binary gate, not a continuous model.
portability_matrix phi values require both platforms to be measured. SYCL cross-platform phi is currently unavailable for E1/E3/E4/E6/E7 (SYCL compiler unavailable on RTX 5060 in those experiments).
